title: OpticalMosaicRecipe
description:
A SEPAL Optical Mosaic recipe model. Produces a cloud-free composite mosaic from optical satellite imagery (Landsat 4â€“9, Sentinel-2) over a defined area, season, and processing options.

**What this recipe is for.** Optical sensors record reflected sunlight across the visible/NIR/SWIR spectrum. Unlike radar, they cannot see through clouds â€” every scene contains some cloudy pixels that must be detected and replaced. The recipe collects all scenes in the configured season, masks clouds/shadows/snow per-pixel, and composites the remaining clear pixels into a single per-pixel best-guess mosaic.

**Bands produced.** Common optical bands across the selected sources (typically `blue, green, red, nir, swir1, swir2`) plus per-sensor extras (Sentinel-2 red-edge bands, Landsat thermal, etc.) and metadata bands (`dayOfYear, daysFromTarget`). Spectral indexes (`ndvi, ndmi, evi, nbr, â€¦`) can be requested as outputs.

**Output values.** With Surface Reflectance correction (`SR` in `corrections`), reflectance values are scaled to [0, 10000] (representing 0â€“100 % reflectance Ã— 10000); without it, top-of-atmosphere reflectance in the same scaling. Spectral indexes are similarly scaled by 10000 (so NDVI in [-10000, 10000]).

This schema describes the persisted `model` object of an *initialized* recipe. It does not cover UI state, recipe id, or recipe metadata. Cross-field constraints that JSON Schema cannot express are enforced by the companion rules.

aoi.description:
Area of Interest. Optical mosaic supports an Earth Engine table (country/area selection or user-supplied table) or a hand-drawn polygon. The asset-bounds variant is not valid for this recipe â€” optical mosaic does not wrap a single asset.

See the AOI schema definitions for variant details. Required from the user; there is no sensible default.

scenes.description:
Manually selected scenes per scene area, used only when `sceneSelectionOptions.type === 'SELECT'`. Keys are scene-area ids (Sentinel-2 MGRS tiles or Landsat WRS-2 path/row strings); values are arrays of selected scene descriptors. Leave as `{}` (or omit) for `ALL` mode.

The internal scene-descriptor shape is intentionally not constrained here â€” it is populated by the GUI's manual scene picker and round-tripped opaquely by the recipe pipeline. The LLM should not synthesize scene descriptors; it should choose `sceneSelectionOptions.type='ALL'` instead.

dates.title: OpticalMosaicDates
dates.description:
Time scope for the mosaic. Optical mosaic uses a *seasonal* model: a target date plus a season window that may extend across a year boundary, optionally repeated across multiple years.

- `targetDate` â€” the date the mosaic represents. Scenes acquired closer to this date receive higher weight in MEDOID compositing and target-date-weighted scene ranking.
- `seasonStart`, `seasonEnd` â€” the **half-open** seasonal window `[seasonStart, seasonEnd)`. **`seasonStart` is inclusive; `seasonEnd` is exclusive** â€” scenes acquired ON `seasonEnd` are NOT included. To capture the full calendar year 2024 set `seasonStart='2024-01-01'` and `seasonEnd='2025-01-01'` (NOT `'2024-12-31'`). Wraparound is allowed: `seasonStart='2024-12-01'`, `seasonEnd='2025-03-01'` selects Decemberâ€“February. `seasonStart` must lie within the year ending at `targetDate`; `seasonEnd` must lie within the year following `targetDate`.
- `yearsBefore` / `yearsAfter` â€” number of additional past/future years of the same season to include. `yearsBefore=2, yearsAfter=0` with a 2024 dry-season window pulls 2022, 2023, 2024 dry seasons.

**Coverage.** Earliest valid `targetDate` is **1982-08-22** (Landsat 4 launch â€” start of useful Landsat-MSS-class coverage). Sentinel-2 imagery is only available from 2015 onward, so a pre-2015 target with a Sentinel-2-only source will silently produce empty mosaics. Future `targetDate` values are accepted (the mosaic represents an aspirational seasonal snapshot â€” late-season scenes will be missing if they have not yet been acquired).

**Multi-year aggregation tradeoffs.** Adding `yearsBefore` / `yearsAfter` is the easiest way to fill gaps in cloudy regions, but blurs the temporal meaning of the mosaic â€” pixels may come from any of the included years. Use sparingly when the user wants a snapshot; use freely (5â€“25 years) when the user wants a long-term composite for stable land cover.

type.description: Optional mode tag. Only accepted value is `YEARLY_TIME_SCAN`. The EE backend ignores this field, and the GUI's dates panel strips it on every save â€” so most real-world models do not have it. Provided for backwards compatibility only; the LLM does not need to set it.

targetDate.description: Date the mosaic represents. Scenes nearer this date are preferred when ranking. Must be on or after `1982-08-22` (Landsat-4 launch). Future targetDates are allowed.

seasonStart.description: **Inclusive** start of the eligibility window for scenes (each year of the season). Scenes acquired ON `seasonStart` are included. Must be within `[targetDate âˆ’ 1 year + 1 day, targetDate]`. For a calendar-year mosaic, set this to January 1 of `targetDate`'s year.

seasonEnd.description: **Exclusive** end of the eligibility window (each year of the season). Scenes acquired ON `seasonEnd` are NOT included â€” set `seasonEnd` to the day AFTER the last day you want covered. For the full calendar year 2024 use `2025-01-01`, NOT `2024-12-31`. Must be within `[targetDate + 1 day, targetDate + 1 year]`.

yearsBefore.description: Additional past years of the same season to include. `0` (default) uses only the season anchored on `targetDate`'s year. Values up to 25 are allowed; the GUI snaps to typical values 0, 1, 2, 3, 5, 10, 25.

yearsAfter.description: Additional future years of the same season to include. `0` (default) uses only the season anchored on `targetDate`'s year. Values up to 25 are allowed; the GUI snaps to typical values 0, 1, 2, 3, 5, 10, 25. Future years beyond today simply contribute no scenes.

sources.title: OpticalMosaicSources
sources.description:
Which satellites to draw scenes from, and the maximum scene-level cloud cover for filtering before per-pixel cloud masking. The recipe groups data sets under their source: `LANDSAT` (Landsat 4-9 missions, Tier 1 by default; Tier 2 variants prefixed `_T2`) and `SENTINEL_2`.

**Choosing sources.** Landsat (30 m) has a 1982-onward archive and is the canonical choice for long-term studies. Sentinel-2 (10 m) starts in 2015, has a 5-day revisit, and produces visibly sharper mosaics where applicable. Combining both increases scene density at the cost of cross-sensor calibration overhead and forces `sceneSelectionOptions.type='ALL'`.

**Tier 2 (`_T2`) variants** include scenes that failed the Tier 1 quality checks (typically due to incomplete metadata or geometric/radiometric anomalies). They add coverage but with lower confidence per scene; use only when Tier 1 leaves gaps you can't fill another way.

cloudPercentageThreshold.description:
Maximum scene-level cloud cover (percent) for a scene to be considered. The cloud cover value comes from each scene's metadata (CLOUD_COVER for Landsat, CLOUDY_PIXEL_PERCENTAGE for Sentinel-2) â€” it is the producer's per-scene estimate, not a per-pixel measurement. Scenes above this threshold are dropped before any per-pixel cloud masking.

- `100` â€” accept every scene; rely entirely on per-pixel cloud masking.
- `75` (default) â€” drop the very cloudiest scenes but keep most. Reasonable middle ground.
- `25â€“50` â€” be selective. Useful when scene density is high (many revisits) and quality matters more than coverage.
- `0â€“10` â€” only nearly-cloud-free scenes. Use only in regions with abundant clear acquisitions.

The GUI snaps to typical values 0, 10, 25, 50, 75, 90, 100; any integer in [0, 100] is accepted.

dataSets.description: Map of source group â†’ array of dataset ids selected from that group. At least one group must be present, and at least one dataset within it. When BOTH groups are present, cross-sensor band calibration is required (`compositeOptions.corrections` must include `CALIBRATE`) and manual scene selection is disabled (`sceneSelectionOptions.type` is forced to `ALL`).

LANDSAT.description: Landsat missions to include. `LANDSAT_9` (2021+), `LANDSAT_8` (2013+), `LANDSAT_7` (1999+), `LANDSAT_TM` (Landsat 4-5, 1982-2012). The `_T2` variants are Tier 2 collections â€” the same scenes that did not meet Tier 1 quality criteria; lower per-scene confidence but extra coverage. Default `['LANDSAT_9', 'LANDSAT_8']`.

SENTINEL_2.description: Sentinel-2 mission. The only valid entry is `SENTINEL_2` (the constellation is treated as a single dataset; coverage from 2015 onward, 10 m bands).

sceneSelectionOptions.title: OpticalMosaicSceneSelectionOptions
sceneSelectionOptions.description:
How scenes from the eligible pool are chosen. Two modes:

- `ALL` â€” use every eligible scene. The composite is computed from all per-pixel observations; per-scene quality is irrelevant beyond the `cloudPercentageThreshold` filter. This is the default and the only mode usable with multiple source groups.
- `SELECT` â€” rank scenes per scene-area (a Sentinel-2 MGRS tile or Landsat WRS-2 path/row) and either auto-pick the top-N or let the user manually choose them via the scene-picker UI. Available only with a single source group; selected scenes are persisted in the top-level `scenes` object.

The LLM should virtually always choose `ALL`. Manual scene selection is a workflow that depends on the GUI; an LLM has no useful way to populate `scenes`.

type.description: Scene selection mode. `ALL` uses every eligible scene (default and recommended). `SELECT` enables manual scene picking; only valid with a single source group.

targetDateWeight.description: Trade-off between target-date proximity and cloud-free quality when ranking scenes for `SELECT` mode. `0` = pure cloud-free preference (default), `0.5` = balanced, `1` = pure target-date preference. Has no effect when `type='ALL'`.

compositeOptions.title: OpticalMosaicCompositeOptions
compositeOptions.description:
Per-scene processing and per-pixel compositing options. Roughly grouped by purpose:

- **Corrections** (`corrections`, `brdfMultiplier`) â€” apply Surface Reflectance, BRDF, cross-sensor band calibration before compositing.
- **Sentinel-2 overlap handling** (`orbitOverlap`, `tileOverlap`) â€” deduplicate redundant Sentinel-2 acquisitions.
- **Per-pixel cloud masking** (`includedCloudMasking` plus per-method tuning) â€” multiple methods can run; the masks are unioned. `cloudBuffer` enlarges every cloud detection.
- **Other masks** (`snowMasking`, `holes`).
- **Quality filters** (`filters`) â€” per-scene scoring of shadow/haze/seasonality, used to drop the worst scenes after masking.
- **Compositing method** (`compose`) â€” how surviving per-pixel observations are reduced into a single value.

Many fields are conditionally meaningful â€” read each field's description and the cross-field rules.

advanced.description: UI hint â€” was the panel last edited in 'advanced' mode. Has no effect on processing. Safe to omit.

corrections.description:
Pre-compositing corrections applied to every scene.

- `SR` â€” Surface Reflectance. Atmospheric correction is applied (Landsat: USGS Collection 2 SR; Sentinel-2: ESA L2A). Strongly recommended for any quantitative analysis (indexes, classification, regression). Default includes `SR`.
- `BRDF` â€” Bidirectional Reflectance Distribution Function correction. Removes view-angle-dependent reflectance variation across a scene; especially noticeable in Sentinel-2 east-west scene edges. Cheap to compute and almost always worth it. Default includes `BRDF`.
- `CALIBRATE` â€” cross-sensor band calibration. Required when combining Landsat AND Sentinel-2 sources; uses TOA reflectance to adjust band differences so the composite is internally consistent. **Must NOT be combined with `SR`** (cross-sensor calibration uses TOA values; the SEPAL implementation does not derive calibrated SR). Implies multiple source groups in `sources.dataSets`.

Default `['SR', 'BRDF']`.

brdfMultiplier.description: Strength multiplier for the BRDF correction. The c-factor adjustment is multiplied by this value before being applied; `1` is the textbook correction, `4` (default) provides a moderately stronger normalization that empirically produces flatter Sentinel-2 mosaics. Used only when `corrections` includes `BRDF`. Must be > 0; very high values (>10) over-correct and produce visible color shifts.

filters.description:
Per-scene quality filters that drop the lowest-percentile scenes by some score. Each filter computes a score per scene (after cloud masking) and keeps scenes above the chosen percentile of the score distribution.

Default `[]` (no filters). Filters tighten the scene pool at the cost of coverage; use sparingly when the result has too many low-quality contributors.

type.description:
Score the filter ranks scenes by.

- `SHADOW` â€” fraction of pixels classified as shadow. Drops the shadowiest scenes.
- `HAZE` â€” atmospheric haze score from blue-band brightness. Drops the haziest scenes. **Not available with `corrections` containing `SR`** (the SR correction has already removed atmosphere).
- `NDVI` â€” scene-mean NDVI. Drops the lowest-vegetation scenes â€” useful in vegetated AOIs where low-NDVI scenes typically indicate cloud/haze contamination missed by other masks.
- `DAY_OF_YEAR` â€” closeness to `targetDate`. Drops scenes farthest from the target. Useful for sharpening seasonality.

percentile.description: Percentile threshold. `0` is off (no filtering); `100` is most aggressive (keeps only the single best scene). Default when adding a filter via the GUI is `50` (drop the worse half). The GUI snaps to 0, 10, 25, 50, 75, 90, 100.

orbitOverlap.description:
How to handle Sentinel-2 orbit overlap â€” adjacent relative orbits cover overlap strips, producing duplicate observations from different look angles.

- `KEEP` (default) â€” keep both. Maximum scene density; can introduce subtle striping at orbit boundaries due to view-angle differences.
- `REMOVE` â€” keep only one observation per overlap. Removes striping at the cost of fewer observations per pixel.

Ignored when `sources.dataSets` does not include `SENTINEL_2`.

tileOverlap.description:
How to handle Sentinel-2 MGRS tile overlap â€” adjacent tiles share overlap zones with two copies of the same data.

- `KEEP` â€” keep both. Pixels in overlap zones are processed twice, harmless but wasteful.
- `QUICK_REMOVE` (default) â€” fast deduplication using a per-tile geometry test. Cheap; usually sufficient.
- `REMOVE` â€” exact per-pixel deduplication. More expensive but more thorough.

Ignored when `sources.dataSets` does not include `SENTINEL_2`.

includedCloudMasking.description:
Per-pixel cloud-masking methods to apply. The masks from each enabled method are unioned (a pixel is masked if any method flags it). Each method has its own tuning fields below.

Availability constraints:
- `sepalCloudScore` â€” universal; works with all sources.
- `landsatCFMask` â€” requires `sources.dataSets.LANDSAT`.
- `sentinel2CloudScorePlus` â€” requires `sources.dataSets.SENTINEL_2`.
- `sentinel2CloudProbability` â€” requires `sources.dataSets.SENTINEL_2`. Older method, largely superseded by Cloud Score+; included for backwards compatibility.
- `pino26` â€” requires Sentinel-2 ONLY (no Landsat) AND `corrections` must NOT include `SR`. Aggressive empirical mask using TOA bands.

Default `['sepalCloudScore', 'landsatCFMask', 'sentinel2CloudScorePlus']` â€” matches the GUI's MODERATE preset.

sentinel2CloudProbabilityMaxCloudProbabi.description: Threshold for the older Sentinel-2 cloud probability mask: pixels with cloud probability above this are masked. Lower â†’ more aggressive masking, more holes; higher â†’ looser masking, more residual clouds. Default `65`. Used only when `includedCloudMasking` contains `sentinel2CloudProbability`.

sentinel2CloudScorePlusBand.description: Which Cloud Score+ band to threshold on. `cs` is the raw clear-sky score; `cs_cdf` is the CDF-normalized variant â€” interpretable across regions. `cs_cdf` (default) is recommended; use `cs` only when comparing with prior work that used it. Used only when `includedCloudMasking` contains `sentinel2CloudScorePlus`.

sentinel2CloudScorePlusMaxCloudProbabili.description: Threshold (in %) for Cloud Score+ masking â€” pixels whose chosen Cloud Score+ band indicates more than this much cloud are masked. Lower is more aggressive. Default `45`. Used only when `includedCloudMasking` contains `sentinel2CloudScorePlus`.

landsatCFMaskCloudMasking.description: Aggressiveness of CFMask cloud-pixel masking on Landsat. `OFF` keeps everything CFMask flagged as cloud; `MODERATE` (default) masks pixels with high cloud confidence; `AGGRESSIVE` masks any pixel CFMask flagged. Used only when `includedCloudMasking` contains `landsatCFMask`.

landsatCFMaskCloudShadowMasking.description: Aggressiveness of CFMask cloud-shadow masking on Landsat. Same OFF/MODERATE/AGGRESSIVE semantics. Default `MODERATE`. Used only when `includedCloudMasking` contains `landsatCFMask`.

landsatCFMaskCirrusMasking.description: Aggressiveness of CFMask cirrus masking on Landsat (cirrus = thin high cloud, usually only detectable on the cirrus band). Default `MODERATE`. Used only when `includedCloudMasking` contains `landsatCFMask`.

landsatCFMaskDilatedCloud.description: Whether to also mask CFMask's dilated-cloud pixels â€” the buffer of edge pixels CFMask flags around clouds. `REMOVE` (default) is conservative; `KEEP` retains the dilated buffer at the cost of more cloud-edge contamination. Used only when `includedCloudMasking` contains `landsatCFMask`.

sepalCloudScoreMaxCloudProbability.description: Threshold for the SEPAL cloud-score mask â€” pixels whose computed score exceeds this are masked. Lower is more aggressive. Default `30`. Used only when `includedCloudMasking` contains `sepalCloudScore`.

cloudBuffer.description:
Buffer (in meters) added around every detected cloud pixel before masking. The cloud-pixel detectors miss cloud edges; a buffer dilates each detection to catch them.

- `0` (default) â€” no buffer.
- `120` â€” moderate buffer (~4 Sentinel-2 pixels, ~4 Landsat pixels). Reduces cloud-edge contamination.
- `600` â€” aggressive buffer. Use only in heavily cloud-affected scenes; produces large holes.

Discrete enum because the GUI's Form.Buttons exposes only these three values; the implementation accepts only these three.

holes.description:
How to handle pixels with no clear observations.

- `ALLOW` (default) â€” leave such pixels masked (transparent in the output). Visualizations show holes where no cloud-free data was available. The right choice when downstream consumers need to know which pixels are reliable.
- `PREVENT` â€” fill holes by relaxing the cloud mask in the holiest places (lowest-quality observations are accepted to keep coverage). Produces a hole-free mosaic at the cost of degraded quality where data was scarce. Useful for visual products and basemaps.

snowMasking.description:
Whether to mask snow pixels.

- `ON` (default) â€” snow is masked along with clouds. Right for most thematic mapping (snow is rarely the target signal and confuses cloud detectors).
- `OFF` â€” keep snow. Use for snow/ice studies, cryosphere monitoring, or any analysis where snow is the signal.

compose.description:
Per-pixel reduction across surviving observations.

- `MEDOID` (default) â€” pick the single observation per pixel whose multi-band vector is closest to the median across all observations. Preserves spectral integrity (no cross-band averaging artifacts) and per-pixel temporal coherence (the chosen observation is a real scene, not a synthetic blend). Recommended for indexes, classification, regression.
- `MEDIAN` â€” per-band median. Smoothes noise but breaks spectral integrity (different bands may come from different scenes' best estimates) and produces no per-pixel acquisition date. Mostly used for visual products where smoothness matters more than fidelity.

allOf.description: brdfMultiplier required when corrections includes BRDF.
Cloud Score+ tuning required when includedCloudMasking contains sentinel2CloudScorePlus.
Landsat CFMask tuning required when includedCloudMasking contains landsatCFMask.
sepalCloudScoreMaxCloudProbability required when includedCloudMasking contains sepalCloudScore.
